summary.headline: CI is currently fully broken end-to-end: the dependency-install step cannot resolve constellation-node-sdk, and even if that is bypassed locally, tests/conftest.py's import chain fails immediately so 0% of the pytest suite can collect. Both must be fixed before any lint/type/test signal from CI can be trusted.

checks.summary: pip cannot resolve 'constellation-node-sdk>=1.0.0' from any configured index; it is not published to PyPI and this repo carries no private index / vendored copy, so a clean-room `pip install -e ".[dev]"` (exactly what ci.yml's 'Install package with dev dependencies' step runs) fails before any lint/type/test step ever executes.

errors.message: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement constellation-node-sdk>=1.0.0 (from constellation-gate) (from versions: none)
No matching distribution found for constellation-node-sdk>=1.0.0

notes: Reproduced with --ignore-installed so already-installed local copies (e.g. an editable install cloned from the Quantum-L9/Gate_SDK repo) do not mask the failure.
The real SDK source lives in the separate Quantum-L9/Gate_SDK GitHub repo (package name constellation-node-sdk) but is not published anywhere pip can reach it from.

checks.summary: pre-commit's mypy hook environment cannot be built: same unresolvable constellation-node-sdk dependency as the CI install step. `pre-commit run` (and therefore `pre-commit run --all-files` / commit-time hooks) fails on environment setup before mypy ever runs.

errors.message: additional_dependencies entry 'constellation-node-sdk>=1.0.0' cannot be resolved when pre-commit builds the mypy hook's isolated environment

notes: Unlike ci.yml (which runs `mypy src` directly in an environment that already has constellation-node-sdk installed via the package's own install step), pre-commit's mypy mirror hook always provisions its own isolated venv from additional_dependencies, so it is independently broken even if step 1 above is fixed by vendoring/publishing the SDK.

pyproject.toml pins mypy>=1.11.0 with no upper bound, so this environment resolved a materially newer mypy release than the pre-commit/mirrors-mypy rev pinned in .pre-commit-config.yaml; see the version-drift finding for details.

checks.command: static comparison of .pre-commit-config.yaml revs vs resolved versions
checks.summary: 2 tool(s) have unpinned upper bounds in pyproject.toml causing version drift against the pins used by .pre-commit-config.yaml.

errors.message: pre-commit pins an older ruff release than pyproject.toml's unbounded 'ruff>=0.6.0' resolves to; local/CI lint results and the pre-commit hook can disagree.

pre-commit pins an older mypy release than pyproject.toml's unbounded 'mypy>=1.11.0' resolves to; stricter checks in newer mypy releases can surface errors in CI/local `mypy src` runs that the pinned pre-commit hook (when its environment can even build) would not.

pytest_deep_scan.description: One-off manual deeper scan performed during this discovery session. tests/conftest.py cannot be imported (see pytest-collection check), which blocks 100% of automated test collection. To see what additional errors exist *underneath* that blocker, two local-only patches were applied temporarily (never committed) to satisfy the two missing symbols, and the suite was then run end-to-end with a per-test timeout guard.

temporary_patches_applied.issue: constellation_gate.observability.__init__ imports 'observe_execution_latency' from this module, but the function was never defined here even though the EXECUTION_LATENCY_SECONDS Histogram it should record to already exists. docs/archive/gate-md-ingestion-export.md shows the original harvested source did define it; it was dropped when the module was published to src/, making every import of constellation_gate fail immediately.
temporary_patches_applied.suggested_fix:
def observe_execution_latency(*, action: str, seconds: float) -> None:
    EXECUTION_LATENCY_SECONDS.labels(action=action.strip().lower()).observe(seconds)

temporary_patches_applied.issue: constellation_gate.resilience.__init__ imports 'RetryDecision' and expects RetryPolicy to expose max_attempts/delay_seconds/backoff_multiplier/retryable_exceptions/decision_for(); the committed file is a stale two-parameter stub (max_attempts, delay_seconds only, no RetryDecision dataclass, no decision_for/backoff support) that predates the real implementation. docs/archive/gate-md-ingestion-export.md contains the complete intended implementation.
temporary_patches_applied.suggested_fix: Replace the file contents with the RetryPolicy/RetryDecision implementation recorded at docs/archive/gate-md-ingestion-export.md (search for 'class RetryDecision').

additional_collection_error.note: Fails to collect even after both patches above; likely references another export that still does not exist -- needs its own follow-up investigation once the two blocking issues are fixed.

representative_root_causes.error: TypeError: TransportPacket.derive() got an unexpected keyword argument 'idempotency_key'
representative_root_causes.note: constellation-node-sdk's TransportPacket.derive() signature does not accept idempotency_key; Gate's test/production code assumes a newer/different SDK contract than the installed constellation-node-sdk 1.0.0 exposes (contract drift between Gate and its SDK dependency).
Dispatcher.dispatch()'s hop-append call in src/constellation_gate/routing/dispatch.py builds a TransportHop keyed by the wrong packet id for this code path.

pytest_deep_scan.caveat: This sub-report was produced by hand during the discovery session (not by this checked-in script, which stays strictly read-only) because getting past the conftest ImportError requires source modifications that a pure discovery tool must not silently apply. Re-run manually after the two blocking fixes above are applied to keep this section current.
